response:
a dedicated gardener has a spacious garden that she divides into smaller sections for planting different types of flowers she wants to create a rectangular flower bed that is twice as long as it is wide to enhance the garden's beauty and accommodate various plants she decides to add a walkway around the flower bed which increases the total area by 72 square feet the width of the walkway is uniform all around the flower bed

the flower bed has a width of x and length of 2x the total area of the flower bed and the walkway can be represented as x times 2x plus the area of the walkway which is added around the flower bed the walkway adds an additional layer of width w around the flower bed so the total dimensions become 2x + 2w and x + 2w the total area is then (2x + 2w) times (x + 2w) this gives the equation for the total area of the flower bed and the walkway in terms of x and w

the equation for the total area of the flower bed and the walkway is (2x + 2w)(x + 2w) and when x is 6 the total area is 72 square feet solving this equation gives the width of the walkway w the solution is w = 2 feet
